program_chi: 全球研究社會科學學士
learning_outcomes:
Learning outcome 1. Major Programme: Knowledge Upon completion of this Programme, students will have
gained: • a basic
understanding of the economic, cultural, political, and environmental dimensions
of globalization; • a strong
appreciation of the challenges of
globalization and the opportunities it presents for the growth and development of countries; • a solid grasp
of the issues related to governance and conflicts in global contexts, geopolitics
and the world in the
media; • a working
knowledge of social science research methods, including qualitative research,
surveys, mathematical modeling, computer simulation, and data mining; and • specialized
knowledge in at least two of the following five concentration areas: Global
Media, Cultures and Societies,
World Politics, Sustainability and Environment, and Global Economy. Skills Students of this Programme will have acquired skills in: • analytical
and creative thinking through its emphasis on interdisciplinary and
multicultural studies, and analyzing
the complexity of global phenomena from multilateral perspectives; • multilingual
and multicultural settings through studies in a foreign language and resident
study overseas; • cross-cultural
interactions and managing culturally diverse project teams through mandatory
internships in multinational
organizations; and • interdisciplinary
and collaborative research through group research projects supervised by
researchers from a wide
range of disciplines. Values and Attitudes Students will have acquired the following
values/attitudes: • global
consciousness and awareness of cultural differences; • appreciation
of the unique strengths of certain foreign cultures; and • the mentality
to think globally and act locally.
2. Minor
Programme: Upon the completion of the Minor Programme, students will
have demonstrated basic understanding of fundamental issues related to the
subject, acquired knowledge of some aspects of the subject with sufficient
exposure and at a suitable level to allow further study in this subject or
related subjects or to pursue a related career.
study_scheme:
Global Studies (4-year programme) Global Studies Applicable to students admitted in 2025-26
Major Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 60 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
GLSD1001 and any two courses, offered
by two different Programmes/Departments/Schools, chosen from the following:
ARCH1001, 1002, 1003, COMM1110, 1120,
1150, 1500, DSPS1001, 1003, 1004, ECON1210, 1220, 2011, GPAD1020, 1076,
1077, GRMD1302, 1401, 1402, PSYC1000, 1630, SOCI1001, 1201, SOSC1001, 1002,
1003, SOWK1001, 1113, 1114, URSP1001
2.  |  Required Courses:
(a)  |  GLSD1003, 2001  |  6
(b)  |  Resident Study Overseas:  |  9
Students are required to study
overseas for one academic term in a partner institution via an exchange
programme administered by the Office of Academic Links or the Colleges, and
complete at least 9 units of courses related to Global Studies approved by
the Programme Director. Grades obtained in these courses are not included
in the calculation of GPA. Students who are unable to obtain the required
units from partner institutions must make up the shortfall by completing
appropriate courses at CUHK, subject to the approval of the Programme
Director.
(c)  |  Language Courses:  |  6
Students are required to take 6 units
of language courses other than those on Chinese (Cantonese/Putonghua) or
English. Non-local students whose native language is not Chinese or English
must take courses on a language that is approved by the Programme Director.
(d)  |  Capstone Courses:  |  6
Choose one of the following:
(GLSD4001 and 4002) or (GLSD4003 and
4004)
3.  |  Elective Courses[a]:  |  21
(a)  |  Global Media and Popular
Culture COMM2100#, 2590#, 2922#, 2930#, 4191#,
GDRS2002#, 3021#, GLSD2101, 2201, 3101, 3102, 3201, 3303, GPAD1016#, 3071#,
3220#, URSP2030#, 2050#
(b)  |  Global Economy ECON3320#, 3350#, 3570#, GLEF3020#,
3030#, 3040#, 4020#, GLSD2501, 3501, 3511, 4102
(c)  |  World Politics DSPS3310#, GLSD3106, 3301, 3302, 3311,
3402, 3403, 3404, 3405, 3411, 4401, 4402, GPAD1066#, 2120#, 2185#, 2300#, 2345#,
2350#, 2355#, 2370#, 2375#, 2395#, 2450#, 3040#, 3160#, 3161#, 3190#, 3360#,
3365#, 3451#, 4142#, 4225#
(d)  |  Planetary Health DSPS3803#, GLSD2401, 3401, GPAD3360#, GRMD2402#,
2404#, 2501#, 3202#, 3304#, 4203#, 4503#, PSYC3450#, 3660#, 3720#,
URSP2200#, 2800#
(e)  |  Quantitative Methods in Global
Studies DSPS3190#, ECON3140#, GPAD2196#
4.  |  Internship:  |  3
GLSD3601
Total:  |  60
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  GLSD courses at 2000 and above level
as well as those labeled as # will be included in the calculation of Major
GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students may be required to participate
in activities such as field trips and study tours that foster their
learning of global studies.
3.  |  Students must obtain a cumulative GPA
of 3.0 or above in their first year of studies (summer session included) in
order to apply for admission to the Dual Undergraduate Degree Programme
(DDP).
4.  |  Students who participate in the DDP
must first complete at least 42 units of courses of Major requirements at
CUHK. Upon completion of their studies at Waseda, students may apply to
transfer up to 18 units of courses to fulfill the Major
requirements at CUHK, including the 9-unit requirement of Resident Study
Overseas and up to 9 units of the remaining Major requirements.
5.  |  Students are responsible for the extra
costs incurred in relation to the overseas resident study and the
participation of the DDP.
[a]  |  Among the non-GLSD courses, students
can only choose up to three courses under the same subject area.

Minor Programme Title Global Studies
Minor
Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 18 units of courses, with at least
6 units at 3000 or above level, as follows:
Units
1.  |  Elective
Courses:
(a)  |  Any two
courses from the following:  |  6
GLSD1001,
2001, 2101, 2201, 2401, 2501
(b)  |  Any four
courses selected from the following, with at least two GLSD courses at
3000 or above level:  |  12
ARCH3424,
4721, COMM3941, 4191, ECON3320, 3350, 3370, GDRS2002, 3005, 3012, 4011,
GLSD2701, 3101, 3102, 3105, 3106, 3111, 3201, 3211, 3301, 3302, 3303, 3311,
3401, 3402, 3403, 3404, 3405, 3411, 3501, 3511, 3701, 4102, 4104, 4401,
4402, GPAD2120, 2185, 2300, 2345, 2350, 2355, 2370, 2375, 2395, 2450,
3040, 3071, 3160, 3161, 3190, 3220, 3360, 3365, 3451, 4142, 4225,
GRMD2321, 3202, 3203, 3224, 3404, 4402, PSYC3610, SOWK3340, 4550,
URSP2100, 2200, 2400, 3300
Total:  |  18
Explanatory
Notes:
1.  |  Students are
advised not to take courses at 3000 or above level in their first year of
attendance.
2.  |  No more than
6 units of courses taken to fulfill the requirements of the students’ Major
Programme(s) and other Minor Programme(s) respectively can be used to
fulfill the requirement of this Minor Programme.
